description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and a front panel featuring a large black speaker grille on the left. The right side of the front panel is metallic gray, housing two prominent round dials and a vertical rectangular display window, likely for tuning or frequency indication. The wood grain texture is visible on all sides except the front panel, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The dials are raised and appear to be made of plastic or metal, designed for manual adjustment. The speaker grille is perforated, allowing sound to emanate clearly from the device. No visible brand or logo is present on the exterior.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a black speaker grille, two round dials, and a vertical display window on a metallic front panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black speaker grille, two round dials, and a vertical display window on the metallic panel.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wooden texture and the protruding dials on the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a featureless wooden surface with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain wooden texture, mirroring the other side but without visible dials.
